headline: ‘Inflation Will Come’ From Iran War, PepsiCo Says
summary: PepsiCo sees the Iran war driving another round of higher prices for consumers.  “Our assumption is that inflation will come,” CFO Steve Schmitt said on an investor call Thursday, though he said it is too soon to know how much.  PepsiCo said it has not had any supply chain issues tied to the Iran war, and that the food and beverage giant maintains six- to 12-month hedges for ingredients and commodities like oats, corn and aluminum.

raw.headline: ‘Inflation Will Come’ From Iran War, PepsiCo Says
raw.summary: PepsiCo sees the Iran war driving another round of higher prices for consumers.  “Our assumption is that inflation will come,” CFO Steve Schmitt said on an investor call Thursday, though he said it is too soon to know how much.  PepsiCo said it has not had any supply chain issues tied to the Iran war, and that the food and beverage giant maintains six- to 12-month hedges for ingredients and commodities like oats, corn and aluminum.

headline: PepsiCo's sales jump after it cuts prices
summary: PepsiCo's decision to cut prices and some new snacks boosted demand in the first quarter.  Revenue jumped 8.5% to $19.44 billion in the January-March period compared to the same period a year ago, the Purchase, New York, company said Thursday.  PepsiCo began cutting prices on value brands like Chester's and Santitas last spring to win back customers exasperated by years of price hikes.

headline: S&P 500 Settles At New Record High: Investor Sentiment Improves, Fear & Greed Index Moves To 'Greed' Zone
summary: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index showed improvement in market sentiment. Stocks rose, with S&P and Nasdaq hitting all-time highs. Trump predicts end to conflict and lower oil prices. Morgan Stanley and Bank of America report strong earnings. U.S. import and export prices rose, while the NY Empire State Manufacturing Index beat expectations. Most sectors on S&P closed negative, except for tech and consumer stocks. Dow down, S&P up, and Nasdaq jumps. Earnings results expected from PepsiCo, Netflix, and Abbott Labs. The Fear & Greed Index measures market sentiment based on fear and greed, with a current reading of 56.5 indicating greed. Index ranges from 0 to 100.

headline: Will S&P 500 Open Up Or Down On April 16?
summary: The S&P 500 rose 0.8% on Wednesday to close at a record 7,022.95, extending its recent rally as investors continued to bet that the U.S.-Iran war may soon draw to a close.
